purpose: Abstract design observations only; no source question text, values, artwork, branding or layouts are stored.
reasoning: A short accessible setup can conceal a non-obvious invariant, symmetry, conservation rule or counting shortcut.
Strong intermediate questions connect at least two facts before routine calculation becomes useful.
Strong senior questions require a chain of constraints and reward reframing over large arithmetic.
Distractors should correspond to plausible incomplete models, not random nearby numbers.
visuals: A diagram earns its space by carrying information that would be awkward or revealing in prose.
Text and diagram should divide the information load cleanly.
Restrained line work, sparse colour and generous whitespace keep mathematical relationships legible.
paperStyle: Warm white question field, serif question copy and blue question numbering.
Thin horizontal separators and evenly distributed A–E choices.
Diagrams sit beside or below the prompt according to their natural aspect ratio.
